text.title: GitLab Issue Boards Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the GitLab Issue Boards methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Workflow state MUST be modelled as scoped labels (workflow::*), not as regular labels; mixing scoped and unscoped state labels is forbidden.
rule.rationale: scoped labels enforce mutual exclusion; regular labels do not, so issues end up in multiple workflow columns at once.

rule.statement: Every In-Progress-style column MUST have an explicit WIP limit displayed on the board; columns without WIP limits are flagged as untracked.
rule.rationale: WIP limits are the whole point of Kanban; without them the board is just a wish list.

rule.statement: Labels MUST be seeded via the seed_labels.py script and tracked in version control; ad-hoc label creation in the UI is forbidden.
rule.rationale: ad-hoc labels drift between projects and break cross-project boards.

rule.statement: Priority (priority::*) and type (type::*) MUST be modelled as separate scoped-label sets; a single label encoding both is rejected.
rule.rationale: mixed priority/type labels prevent filtering and break automation.

rule.statement: Each board MUST ship with a README documenting column entry/exit criteria; un-documented boards are flagged for cleanup.
rule.rationale: without documented entry/exit criteria, every team member uses the board differently and signal collapses.

rule.statement: Bug and Feature issues MUST use the templates (issue-template-bug.md, issue-template-feature.md); free-form issues are downgraded to 'triage' until a template is applied.
rule.rationale: free-form issues skip the required context (steps to reproduce, acceptance criteria) and waste reviewer time.
